data.body:
PASS 1 review (E4 — assumption/premise verification): CLEAN — no findings.

Every load-bearing codebase assertion in this plan was probed via Read/Grep and VERIFIED in-tree (training knowledge not relied on; fail-closed applied — none came up unverifiable):

- ✅ The landmine: `src/rebar/grounding/harness.py:281` carries the typo'd `# noqa: BLE0001` (extra `0`) on `except BaseException as exc:` — confirmed verbatim at that exact line (read 270-287). `BLE0001` matches ONLY this site repo-wide, so the noqa is genuinely inert and will newly flag once BLE001 gates. The `KeyboardInterrupt`/`SystemExit`-swallowing premise also holds (it's a bare `except BaseException`).
- ✅ Broad-excepts exist in `grounding/` (resolve.py:605, engine_b.py:164/183, oracle.py:350) and `_engine_support/` (validate.py:89/112) — confirmed present via grep.
- ✅ Stderr-diagnostic prints exist in `_engine_support/` (reads_cli.py, validate.py, resolver.py, reads.py, next_batch.py, bridge_fsck.py) — confirmed; note `grounding/` has NO `print(` sites (grep returned no matches), so "these packages" is accurate only for the `_engine_support` half (a scope nuance, already raised under G1G2/A1, not an E4 premise defect).
- ✅ Allowlist entries the plan removes EXIST exactly as stated: pyproject.toml:213 (`grounding/*` = BLE001), :214 (`grounding/detectors/*` = BLE001), :215 (`_engine_support/*` = BLE001+T201). The allowlist comment (line 211) even names this sweep (d360) as the BLE001 owner.
- ✅ The observability-floor contract the plan invokes (log `exc_info` OR justified `# noqa: BLE001`; KI/SE never swallowed; named `logging.getLogger(__name__)`) is documented in `_logging.py:35-50` — the plan's terminology matches the canonical convention rather than inventing one.

No assertion in the plan is contradicted by the tree, and none is unverifiable. Clean for E4 — empty findings list.
